name: Hilton Bora Bora Nui Resort & Spa

content.verdict: Hilton Bora Bora Nui is the biggest swing on the island and, for honeymooners who want drama without spending Four Seasons money, probably the sharpest deal in French Polynesia. Set on its own private island (Motu Toopua), the resort has 120 overwater, beachfront and hillside villas spread across a long sweep of turquoise lagoon, with the best sunset views on Bora Bora — you literally watch the sun sink behind Mount Otemanu from your villa deck. The architecture leans traditional Polynesian with peaked thatched roofs, but the interiors were fully refreshed to a modern, light, neutral palette. The showstopper is Hina Spa, carved into a hillside cave — treatment rooms open onto lagoon views and volcanic rock walls. Dining is solid across three restaurants (Iriatai for French fine dining is the honeymoon choice). Service is warm and professional without being stiff, and because of the resort's scale there is more happening than at smaller properties — sunset cruises, shark and ray feeding, a proper infinity pool, a real gym. The honest downside: it's a Hilton, so you will see conference energy creep in during shoulder season, and the beach, while lovely, is narrower than at Le Bora Bora or Pearl Beach. But value-for-dollar, and for couples who want options and activity, this is the smart Bora Bora pick.
content.best_room: The Overwater Villa with Sunset View is the signature honeymoon choice — glass floor panels, outdoor deck with direct lagoon ladder, and a private sunset view of Otemanu that frankly no other resort on Bora Bora can match. If budget allows, the Two-Bedroom Overwater Villa or the Royal Overwater Villa with private plunge pool are worth the jump for a milestone trip. Avoid the Garden Pool Villa for a honeymoon — you came to Bora Bora for the water, not a garden.
caveats: Transfers via resort speedboat cost around $200 per couple round-trip and are not included.
Wi-Fi is complimentary but patchy in overwater villas during weather.
The resort is large (120 villas) so expect other couples around — this is not a hideaway.
Some beach villas are close together; pay up for overwater.
Food and drink prices are brutal even by Bora Bora standards; consider a half-board package.
The main beach has some coral underfoot — water shoes help.

faqs.q: Is Hilton Bora Bora Nui adults-only?
faqs.a: No, it is a family resort, though honeymooners and couples are the dominant guest segment. Kids are present but the resort is large enough that it rarely feels family-centric. If you want strictly no children, consider Rohotu Fare Lodge or Eden Beach.

faqs.q: How does it compare to Four Seasons Bora Bora?
faqs.a: Four Seasons has better beaches, a more intimate feel, and top-tier service. Hilton Bora Bora Nui has better sunset views, a dramatic cave spa, and is 30-40% cheaper for comparable overwater villas. For a first Bora Bora trip with sunset priority, Hilton is arguably the better value.

faqs.q: Can we use Hilton Honors points?
faqs.a: Yes — it is one of the most aspirational Hilton Honors redemptions. Standard awards start around 120,000 points per night but premium rooms (overwater) often cost 150,000-200,000 points. Book 9-12 months out for award availability.

faqs.q: Is the snorkeling good from the villa?
faqs.a: Yes. The house reef in front of the overwater villas is healthy, with reef fish, small rays and occasional blacktip sharks. The coral garden on the edge of the motu is a 5-minute kayak paddle and is some of the best on the island.

Fly to Papeete (PPT), then Air Tahiti to Bora Bora (BOB / Motu Mute) — about 50 minutes. Hilton sends a private speedboat from the airport motu directly to the resort, roughly 30 minutes across the lagoon.

faqs.q: Best time to go for a honeymoon?
faqs.a: May to October is the dry, cooler season and peak — book 6-9 months ahead. September-October hits a sweet spot of good weather, fewer crowds and slightly softer rates. Avoid December-February (wet, humid, occasional cyclones).

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking — Hilton Bora Bora Nui, [dates]

Hi reservations team,

We are planning our honeymoon and would like to book the Overwater Villa with Sunset View for 7 nights, arriving [date].

Could you please confirm:
- Availability and best honeymoon rate for these dates (including any 5th-night-free or honeymoon package)
- Round-trip speedboat transfer cost from Motu Mute airport
- Honeymoon inclusions (in-villa breakfast, bottle of champagne, turndown, etc.)
- Half-board dining plan pricing
- Availability for the Hina Spa couples Tiare ritual on day 4 of our stay
- A private sunset cruise and a beach dinner on the motu on separate evenings

We are celebrating our wedding on [wedding date] and would love any honeymoon touches you can add.

Thank you,
[Names]
